heading: Military judge of a military commission
label: 948j Military judge of a military commission
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 948j, added , , , related to military judges of military commissions, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2604Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 948j, added , , , related to military judges of military commissions, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2604Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006

children.heading: .—Detail of Military Judge
children.label: a .—Detail of Military Judge
children.text: A military judge shall be detailed to each military commission under this chapter. The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations providing for the manner in which military judges are so detailed to military commissions. The military judge shall preside over each military commission to which such military judge has been detailed.

children.heading: .—Eligibility
children.label: b .—Eligibility
children.text: A military judge shall be a commissioned officer of the armed forces who is a member of the bar of a Federal court, or a member of the bar of the highest court of a State, and who is certified to be qualified for duty under (article 26 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice) as a military judge of general courts-martial by the Judge Advocate General of the armed force of which such military judge is a member.section 826 of this title

children.heading: .—Ineligibility of Certain Individuals
children.label: c .—Ineligibility of Certain Individuals
children.text: No person is eligible to act as military judge in a case of a military commission under this chapter if such person is the accuser or a witness or has acted as investigator or a counsel in the same case.

children.heading: .—Consultation With Members; Ineligibility to Vote
children.label: d .—Consultation With Members; Ineligibility to Vote
children.text: A military judge detailed to a military commission under this chapter may not consult with the members except in the presence of the accused (except as otherwise provided in ), trial counsel, and defense counsel, nor may such military judge vote with the members.section 949d of this title

children.heading: .—Other Duties
children.label: e .—Other Duties
children.text: A commissioned officer who is certified to be qualified for duty as a military judge of a military commission under this chapter may perform such other duties as are assigned to such officer by or with the approval of the Judge Advocate General of the armed force of which such officer is a member or the designee of such Judge Advocate General.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on Evaluation of Fitness by Convening Authority
children.label: f .—Prohibition on Evaluation of Fitness by Convening Authority
children.text: The convening authority of a military commission under this chapter may not prepare or review any report concerning the effectiveness, fitness, or efficiency of a military judge detailed to the military commission which relates to such judge’s performance of duty as a military judge on the military commission.
